title: Interaction between ibc and lamvr
abstract: A method of visual information processing includes determining that a block being decoded representing a portion of the visual information is coded using a first coding technique; and decoding the coded representation by using a first decoding technique corresponding to the first coding technique and based on the deciding, by excluding use of a second decoding technique corresponding to a second coding technique; wherein one of the two coding techniques corresponds to a coding technique that uses a same video picture for coding the block being decoded as a reference picture and the other corresponds to a Locally Adaptive Motion Vector Resolution (LAMVR) technique that changes a resolution of motion vectors of a block adaptively.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A visual information decoding method, comprising: determining that a block being decoded representing a portion of the visual information is coded using a first coding technique; and decoding the coded representation by using a first decoding technique corresponding to the first coding technique and based on the deciding, by excluding use of a second decoding technique corresponding to a second coding technique; wherein one of the two coding techniques corresponds to a coding technique that uses a same video picture for coding the block being decoded as a reference picture and the other corresponds to a Locally Adaptive Motion Vector Resolution (LAMVR) technique that changes a resolution of motion vectors of a block adaptively. 2. A visual information decoding method, comprising: determining that a block being decoded representing a portion of an encoded picture of visual information is coded using a coding technique that uses a same video picture for coding the block being decoded as a reference picture; obtaining an indicator signaling a resolution of a motion vector of the block being decoded; and based on the indicator, determining that the block uses a Locally Adaptive Motion Vector Resolution (LAMVR) encoding technique that changes a resolution of motion vectors of a block adaptively. 3. The method of any of claims 1-2, wherein the coding technique that uses a same video picture for coding the block being decoded as a reference picture comprises intra-block copy (IBC) that uses a second block of a same video picture for coding the block being decoded. 4. The method of any of claims 1-2, further comprising, 

 parsing the indictor for the second video coding technique based on the usage of the first video coding technique. 5. The method of any of claims 1-4, wherein the first video coding technology corresponds to a self-referential coding technique, and the second video coding technique corresponds to LAMVR. 6. The method of claims 4 or 5 , further comprising, determining that the current block is coded with the first video coding technology; and excluding the parsing of MV resolutions smaller than 1-pel based on the determination. 7. The method of claim 4 or 5, further comprising, determining that the current block is coded with the first video coding technology; and parsing the MV resolutions no less than 1-pel based on the determination. 8. The method of claim 7, the signaled MV resolutions comprising 1-pel, 2-pel. 9. The method of claim 2, comprising: interpreting the indicator based on whether the block being decoded is coded using the self-referential encoding technique or the block being decoded is coded without using the self- referential encoding technique; decoding the block being decoded by using the interpreted indicator, an self-referential decoding technique corresponding to the self-referential encoding technique and an LAMVR decoding technique corresponding to the LAMVR encoding technique. 10. The method of any of claims 1-2, comprising decoding a first encoded block using the self-referential decoding technique by: obtaining a first plurality of encoded blocks representing the first picture; decoding an initial block in the first plurality of encoded blocks; and 

 upon decoding the initial block, decoding a first block in the first plurabty of encoded blocks based on the initial block. 11. The method of any of claims 1 to 10, comprising decoding a second encoded block representing the second picture associated with the visual information using the LAMVR decoding technique in a second picture by: obtaining a second plurality of encoded blocks including a second block being decoded, the second plurality of encoded blocks representing the second encoded picture; obtaining the motion information for the second encoded block; decoding an indicator signaling a resolution associated with a plurality of motion vectors of the motion information; obtaining a plurality of encoded samples predicting the second block being decoded based on the motion information; and obtaining a plurality of encoded samples representing the second block being decoded based on the plurality of encoded samples predicting the block. 12. The method of any of claims 1 to 11, comprising: avoiding decoding the first block using the LAMVR decoding technique. 13. The method of claim 12, comprising: obtaining a plurality of pictures wherein the plurality of pictures are associated with the visual information and used in decoding a second encoded picture representing the second picture; and when at least one picture in the plurality of pictures is the second picture containing a block being decoded, skipping decoding an indicator signaling a resolution associated with the at least one picture. 14. The method of any of claims 10-11, wherein the first picture is the same as the second picture. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the first block is the same as the second block. 16. The method of claim 12, comprising: obtaining a plurality of reference pictures including wherein the plurality of pictures is associated with the visual information and used in decoding a second encoded picture representing the second picture; and when at least one picture in the plurality of reference pictures is the second picture containing the second block being decoded and at least one third picture in the plurality of reference picture is not the second picture containing the second block being decoded, decoding an indicator signaling a resolution of a motion vector associated with the second picture, wherein the second picture is different from the third picture. 17. The method of any of claims 13 to 16, wherein the block being decoded is decoded based on the third picture and a fourth picture in the plurality of reference pictures, and only one of the third picture and the fourth picture is the second picture containing the second block being encoded. 18. The method of any of claims 13 to 16, wherein the second block being decoded is decoded based on more than two pictures in the plurality of reference pictures including the third picture, wherein at least one picture among the more than two pictures is the second picture containing the second block being decoded and at least one picture among the more than two pictures is different from the second picture. 19. The method of claim 2, comprising: obtaining a first plurality of encoded blocks including the block being decoded, the first plurality of encoded block representing the first picture; determining that the block being decoded representing a portion of an encoded picture of visual information is coded using a self-referential encoding technique that uses a second block of the picture for coding the block being decoded; and decoding the resolution of the motion vector associated with the block being encoded, by interpreting the resolution to mean that the motion vector has an integer sample size. 20. The method of claim 19, comprising: when the determined resolution of the motion vector associated with the block being decoded is smaller than one sample size, avoiding applying the determined resolution to the block being decoded with the self-referential decoding technique. 21. The method of any of claims 19-20, comprising: when the determined resolution of the motion vector associated with the block being decoded is equal or larger than one sample size, applying the determined resolution to the block being decoded with the self-referential decoding technique. 22. A method for encoding a visual information, comprising: encoding the visual information into a plurality of encoded pictures and a plurality of indicators signaling one or more encoding techniques applied, the plurality of indicators comprising aself-referential technique indicator and a Locally Adaptive Motion Vector Resolution (LAMVR) technique indicator, wherein a first block of a first picture associated with the visual information is encoded using a self-referential technique and a second block of a second picture associated with the visual information is encoded using a LAMVR technique, wherein the self-referential technique uses a block of the first picture to encode a different block of the first picture and the LAMVR technique changes the resolution of motion vectors of a block adaptively. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the self-referential technique comprises intra-block copy (IBC). 24. The method of claim 22, further comprising, encoding the indictor for a second video coding technique based on the usage of a first video coding technique. 25. The method of claims 22 or 24, wherein the first video coding technology corresponds to a self-referential coding technique, and the second video coding technique corresponds to LAMVR. 26. The method of claim 24 or 25, further comprising, encoding the current block with the first video coding technology; and excluding signaling the encoding of MV resolutions smaller than 1-pel. 27. The method of claims 24 or 25, further comprising, encoding the current block with the first video coding technology; and excluding signaling the MV resolutions no less than 1-pel. 28. The method of claim 27, the signaled MV resolutions comprising 1-pel, 2-pel. 29. The method of claim 22, comprising encoding the first block using the self-referential technique by: dividing the first picture into a first plurality of blocks; encoding an initial block in the first plurality of blocks; and upon encoding the initial block, encoding a first block in the first plurality of blocks based on the initial block. 30. The method of claims 22-29, comprising encoding the second block using the LAMVR technique in a second picture by: dividing the second picture into a second plurality of blocks including a block being encoded; dividing the block being encoded into a plurality of samples; determining a resolution of a motion vector, wherein the resolution comprises at least two different sample sizes; and encoding an indicator signaling the determined resolution. 31. The method of any of claims 22 to 30, comprising: avoiding encoding the first block using the LAMVR technique. 32. The method of claim 31, comprising: 

 determining a plurality of pictures comprising the third picture, wherein the plurality of pictures are associated with the visual information and used in encoding the second picture; and when at least one picture in the plurality of pictures comprises a block being encoded, skipping encoding an indicator signaling the determined resolution. 33. The method of claim 31, comprising: determining a plurality of pictures comprising the third picture, wherein the plurality of pictures are associated with the visual information and used in encoding the second picture; and when at least one picture in the plurality of pictures comprises a block being encoded and at least one picture in the plurality of picture does not comprises the block being encoded, encoding an indicator signaling a resolution of a motion vector associated with the second picture, wherein the second picture is different from the third picture. 34. The method of claims 1 to 33, comprising assuming that a resolution of a motion vector associated with the first picture has an integer sample size. 35. The method of any of claims 33 or 34, wherein the block being encoded is encoded based on the third picture and a fourth picture, and only one of the third picture and the fourth picture is the second picture containing the block being encoded. 36. The method of claims 33 or 34, wherein the block being encoded is encoded based on more than two pictures including the third picture, wherein at least one picture among the more than two pictures is the second picture containing the block being encoded and at least one picture among the more than two pictures is different from the second picture. 37. The method of any of claims 22 to 36, comprising: dividing the first picture into a first plurality of blocks including a block being encoded; determining a resolution of a motion vector associated with the block being encoded, wherein the resolution of the motion vector has an integer sample size; and encoding the resolution of the motion vector associated with the block being encoded. 38. The method of claim 37, comprising: when the determined resolution of the motion vector associated with the block being encoded is smaller than one sample size, avoiding applying the determined resolution to the block being encoded with the self-referential technique. 39. The method of claims 37-38, comprising: when the determined resolution of the motion vector associated with the block being encoded is equal or larger than one sample size, applying the determined resolution to the block being encoded with the self-referential technique. 40. The method of any of claims 1 to 39, comprising: transmitting from an encoder to a decoder an indicator signaling whether and how to apply the LAMVR technique to a block of the first picture coding using the self-referential technique, the indicator transmitted at a sequence level, a picture level, a slice level, a Coding Tree Unit (CTU) level, a coding unit (CU) level, or a prediction unit level. 41. The method of claim 40, wherein the indicator is signaled in a Sequence Parameter Set (SPS), a Picture Parameter Set (PPS), a Slice Header (SH), a coding tree unit CTU, a coding unit CU or a prediction unit PU. 42. A video processing apparatus comprising a processor configured to implement a method recited in any one or more of claims 1 to 41. 43. A computer readable medium having code stored thereon, the code, upon execution, causing a processor to implement a method recited in any one or more of claims 1 to 41.
